title: Joins seem broken
description:
### What happens?

This join should return no more than 30, but returns 522.

Some columns work as expected, strings etc. but some data types not. Ex. the "Date" column.

```sql
create table t1 as select * from read_csv('covid-small.csv');
create table t2 as select * from read_csv('covid-small.csv');
select count(*) total from t1 inner join t2 on t1."Date"=t2."Date" ;
```

[covid-sample.csv](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/16694014/covid-sample.csv)


### To Reproduce

See above

### OS:

mac

### DuckDB Version:

1

### DuckDB Client:

python

### Full Name:

andrew grosser

### Affiliation:

Sourcetable

### What is the latest build you tested with? If possible, we recommend testing with the latest nightly build.

I have not tested with any build

### Did you include all relevant data sets for reproducing the issue?

Yes

### Did you include all code required to reproduce the issue?

- [X] Yes, I have

### Did you include all relevant configuration (e.g., CPU architecture, Python version, Linux distribution) to reproduce the issue?

- [X] Yes, I have

Ach my bad, bad data.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1 (id INTEGER, date_col DATE, value FLOAT, name VARCHAR(50));
CREATE TABLE t2 (id INTEGER, date_col DATE, value FLOAT, description VARCHAR(100));
SELECT COUNT(*) AS total FROM t1 JOIN t2 ON t1.date_col = t2.date_col;
key_bug_features: Inner join on Date column returns incorrect row count
